validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.integration.test_self_test_run -v passes 2/2 (6.4s, up from 1); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 346/347 with 1 OS-specific skip (45.2s, up from 346 -- 1 new case + wall-clock.md artifact)

checkpoint.summary: unit_057 complete. Wall-clock parallel vs sequential baseline comparison landed: TestWallClockParallelVsSequential runs the Tasklet fixture driver twice (max_concurrent_units=3 parallel, =1 sequential), times both via time.monotonic(), writes fixtures/self-test/wall-clock.md with the data table + ratio narrative. Both runs use worktree fan-out so only batch size differs. Refactored the 126-line in-class _run_one_turn into a standalone _drive_fixture free function shared by both tests. This unit caught 2 runtime bugs during implementation: (1) compute_frontier in select_next_unit.py filters 'completed' but NOT 'failed' -- scope-violated units stay in the frontier forever (worked around at driver level; runtime fix is a separate follow-up). (2) Same-second turns share batch_id from compute_parallel_batch._make_batch_id (1-sec granularity), which combined with bug (1) caused 'git worktree add' to fail with exit 255 on re-dispatch of scope-violated units (branch already exists from preserved worktree). Driver-level frontier filter resolves the symptom. Also flipped scope-violation units to status 'failed' in the phase-graph (both in _run_one_turn and _drive_fixture) -- more correct terminal state than leaving them 'pending'. Updated unit_056's end-state assertion accordingly. Results on this machine: parallel 3 turns @ 1.97s, sequential 6 turns @ 2.00s, ratio 1.02x. The small ratio reflects fake-commit speed; turn count (3 vs 6) is the meaningful signal. Suite 346 -> 347.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_058 (PHASE_013): write skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/POST-MORTEM.md with sections Setup / Results / Papercuts / Follow-ups, embedding the wall-clock.md table + trace.log highlights + the 2 runtime bugs caught in unit_057 (compute_frontier missing-filter for 'failed' status + batch_id 1-sec granularity). Add a link from skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md to the new post-mortem. This is the LAST unit of PHASE_013 -- after it lands: open the phase PR, run code-review, squash-merge per autonomous-merge authorization. PHASE_013 is the final phase of the roadmap; once it closes the development-harness skill upgrade is complete.
